channel.title: Index-Error on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Index-Error on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] C Array Index Out of Bounds — Undefined Behavior Fix
item.description:
[Solution] C Array Index Out of Bounds — Undefined Behavior Fix

An array index out of bounds occurs when your program accesses an array element using an index that is less than zero or greater than or equal to the array’s size. In C, arrays have no built-in bounds checking — accessing an out-of-bounds element reads or writes whatever memory happens to be adjacent, causing undefined behavior, crashes, or silent data corruption.

item.title: [Solution] SQL Duplicate Key Name Error Fix
item.description:
SQL Duplicate Key Name Error Fix

This error occurs when trying to create an index with a name that already exists on the table. The message reads: Duplicate key name 'X'.

Description

Each index on a table must have a unique name within that table. If you try to create an index with a name that’s already in use — whether it’s a user-defined index or an auto-generated one — the database rejects the operation.
